metadata.title: FDA Clinical Trial Guidance Documents — Reference Database for NIX AI
metadata.source: U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER), Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)
metadata.description: Key FDA guidance documents relevant to clinical trial protocol design, conduct, and regulatory submissions.

adaptive_designs.title: Adaptive Designs for Clinical Trials of Drugs and Biologics
key_points: Types of adaptive designs: sample size re-estimation, response-adaptive randomization, seamless Phase II/III, biomarker-adaptive, adaptive dose-finding
Pre-specification of all adaptations in the protocol required
Control of Type I error rate across adaptations
Operational bias prevention through blinding and firewalls
Statistical methods: group sequential, combination tests, Bayesian approaches
DMC role in interim analyses and adaptation decisions
Simulation studies to evaluate operating characteristics recommended
protocol_requirements: Pre-specified adaptation rules in protocol and SAP
Justification for each adaptation
Type I error rate control methodology
Operating characteristics from simulations
DMC charter with decision-making framework
Interim analysis timing and stopping rules

enrichment_designs.title: Enrichment Strategies for Clinical Trials to Support Determination of Effectiveness of Human Drugs and Biological Products
key_points: Practical enrichment: select patients likely to comply, complete study
Prognostic enrichment: select patients with higher event rates
Predictive enrichment: select patients more likely to respond (biomarker-driven)
Biomarker validation requirements for predictive enrichment
Companion diagnostic development when predictive enrichment used

non_inferiority.title: Non-Inferiority Clinical Trials to Establish Effectiveness
key_points: Non-inferiority margin (M2) should preserve at least 50% of active control effect
Historical evidence of active control effect (M1) must be reliable
Constancy assumption: active control effect retained in new trial setting
Both ITT and per-protocol analyses important for NI trials
Fixed margin and synthesis methods for margin selection
Assay sensitivity demonstration required
protocol_requirements: NI margin specified with justification from meta-analysis
M1 (historical effect) and M2 (NI margin) clearly defined
Constancy assumption assessment
Both ITT and PP analysis populations defined
Sensitivity analyses for NI conclusion

master_protocols.title: Master Protocols: Efficient Clinical Trial Design Strategies to Expedite Development of Oncology Drugs and Biologics
key_points: Basket trials: single drug across multiple tumor types/biomarkers
Umbrella trials: multiple drugs in single disease, biomarker-stratified
Platform trials: perpetual protocol allowing arm additions/drops
Shared control arm considerations and concurrent control requirements
Multiplicity adjustment across sub-studies
Governance structure for multi-stakeholder master protocols

complex_innovative_designs.title: Complex Innovative Trial Designs (CID) Pilot Program
key_points: Bayesian adaptive designs, platform trials, response-adaptive randomization
Pre-IND/pre-NDA meetings for CID discussions
Statistical review team engagement for novel designs
Simulation requirements for complex designs

clinical_endpoints.title: Clinical Trial Endpoints for the Approval of Cancer Drugs and Biologics
key_points: Overall survival (OS): gold standard for regular approval in oncology
Progression-free survival (PFS): acceptable for some regular approvals
Objective response rate (ORR): accelerated approval basis
Duration of response (DOR): important complementary endpoint
Disease-free survival (DFS), event-free survival (EFS): adjuvant settings
Patient-reported outcomes (PROs): supportive evidence for approval

surrogate_endpoints.title: Surrogate Endpoint Resources for Drug and Biologic Development
key_points: Table of Surrogate Endpoints used for accelerated and regular approval
Validation requirements: biological plausibility + epidemiological evidence
Risk-benefit considerations when relying on surrogate endpoints
Post-marketing confirmatory study requirements for accelerated approval

patient_reported_outcomes.title: Patient-Reported Outcome Measures: Use in Medical Product Development to Support Labeling Claims
key_points: PRO instrument development and validation pathway
Content validity: qualitative evidence from target population
Construct validity: convergent, discriminant, known-groups
Reliability: test-retest, internal consistency
Meaningful change thresholds (MID/MCID)
FDA review of PRO endpoints in SPAs

digital_health_technologies.title: Digital Health Technologies for Remote Data Acquisition in Clinical Investigations
key_points: DHT as data collection tools in clinical trials
Verification and validation of DHT measurements
Data quality standards for DHT-collected data
Cybersecurity and data integrity requirements

multiplicity.title: Multiple Endpoints in Clinical Trials
key_points: Family-wise error rate (FWER) control strategies
Hierarchical (gatekeeping) testing procedures
Graphical testing approaches (Bonferroni-based)
Split-alpha strategies across dose groups or endpoints
When multiplicity adjustment is required vs. when it is not
Pre-specification of multiple testing strategy in SAP

missing_data.title: Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials: ICH E9 Implementation
key_points: Estimand-based approach to missing data per ICH E9(R1)
Treatment policy strategy: include all data regardless of adherence
Tipping point analyses for sensitivity
Pattern mixture models, selection models
Multiple imputation with appropriate assumptions

bayesian_statistics.title: Guidance for the Use of Bayesian Statistics in Medical Device Clinical Trials
key_points: Prior elicitation methods and justification
Operating characteristics evaluation
Type I error rate control in Bayesian framework
Prior-data conflict detection
Bayesian adaptive designs

subgroup_analyses.title: Evaluation of Sex-Specific Data in Medical Device Clinical Studies
key_points: Pre-specified subgroup analyses by sex, age, race/ethnicity
Interaction tests and interpretation
Subgroup-specific safety and efficacy evaluations

pregnant_women.title: Pregnant Women: Scientific and Ethical Considerations for Inclusion in Clinical Trials

geriatric.title: E7 Studies in Support of Special Populations: Geriatrics

pediatric.title: Pediatric Study Plans: Content of and Process for Submitting Initial Pediatric Study Plans and Amended Initial Pediatric Study Plans

rare_diseases.title: Rare Diseases: Common Issues in Drug Development — Guidance for Industry
key_points: Natural history study importance for rare diseases
Flexible trial designs for small populations
Use of external controls and historical data
Patient registries as data sources
Accelerated approval pathways for rare diseases

safety_reporting_ind.title: Safety Reporting Requirements for INDs and BA/BE Studies
key_points: IND safety report criteria: serious, unexpected, reasonably possible causal relationship
7-day alert reports for fatal or life-threatening events
15-day IND safety reports for all other serious unexpected ADRs
Annual IND safety report requirements
Investigator notification timelines

risk_management.title: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategies (REMS)
key_points: When REMS may be required
REMS components: medication guide, communication plan, ETASU
REMS assessment timelines

rwe_framework.title: Framework for FDA's Real-World Evidence Program
key_points: RWD sources: EHR, claims, registries, DHTs
RWE for new indications and post-approval commitments
Data quality, relevance, and reliability assessment
Study design considerations for RWE
Regulatory grade RWE standards

external_controls.title: Considerations for the Design and Conduct of Externally Controlled Trials for Drug and Biological Products
key_points: When external controls may be appropriate (rare diseases, single-arm oncology)
Data quality requirements for external control data
Propensity score methods for confounding adjustment
Sensitivity analyses for external control comparisons

dct_guidance.title: Decentralized Clinical Trials for Drugs, Biological Products, and Devices
key_points: Remote informed consent (eConsent) procedures
Telemedicine visits replacing in-person site visits
Direct-to-patient (DtP) drug shipment
Home health provider assessments
Local lab and imaging facility use
Data integrity in decentralized settings
IRB/IEC considerations for DCT elements

ai_ml_drug_development.title: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in Drug Development
key_points: AI/ML applications across drug development lifecycle: target identification, clinical trial design, manufacturing, post-market surveillance
Data quality requirements: training data representativeness, bias detection, data integrity
Model transparency and explainability requirements for regulatory submissions
Good Machine Learning Practice (GMLP) principles: valid clinical association, appropriate data representation, reference standards, performance monitoring
Regulatory considerations: predetermined change control plans, human oversight, real-world performance monitoring
AI-derived biomarkers: qualification pathways and validation requirements
Clinical Decision Support (CDS) tools in trials: FDA oversight framework
protocol_requirements: AI/ML model description in protocol if used for endpoint assessment or patient selection
Validation dataset separate from training data
Bias mitigation strategy for AI-assisted decisions
Human oversight plan for AI-supported clinical decisions
Model versioning and change control documentation

ai_ml_medical_devices.title: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML)-Based Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) Action Plan
key_points: Predetermined Change Control Plan framework for AI/ML-based SaMD
Good Machine Learning Practice (GMLP) development
Patient-centered approach for AI/ML transparency
Regulatory science for AI/ML evaluation
Real-world performance monitoring requirements
Total Product Lifecycle (TPLC) approach for AI/ML devices

electronic_records.title: 21 CFR Part 11: Electronic Records; Electronic Signatures
key_points: Requirements for electronic records to be considered trustworthy, reliable, and equivalent to paper
Electronic signature requirements: unique to individual, not reused, verified identity
Audit trail: computer-generated, time-stamped trail of system activities
Validation of computerized systems used to manage electronic records
Access controls: physical and logical restrictions
Applies to clinical trial data management, EDC systems, ePRO platforms
Scope clarification: risk-based approach to Part 11 compliance
protocol_requirements: EDC system Part 11 compliance documented
Audit trail capability for all electronic clinical data
Electronic signature policy for investigators and sponsors
System validation documentation for electronic data capture

project_optimus.title: Project Optimus: Reforming the Dose Optimization and Dose Selection Paradigm in Oncology
key_points: Paradigm shift: maximum tolerated dose (MTD) no longer the default for oncology dose selection
Dose optimisation required: efficacy AND safety data at multiple doses before Phase III
Randomised dose-finding studies expected (not just dose-escalation to MTD)
Applies especially to molecularly targeted therapies and immunotherapies
Dose optimisation data may be required for accelerated approval
FDA may request additional dose optimisation studies during review
Dose reduction strategies and dose modifications should be pre-specified
Exposure-response analysis required to support dose selection rationale
protocol_requirements: Dose selection rationale with exposure-response data
Multiple dose levels evaluated in randomised comparison where feasible
Patient-reported outcomes by dose level to assess tolerability
Dose modification guidelines in protocol
PK/PD modelling supporting selected dose(s)

tissue_agnostic_approvals.title: Tissue Agnostic Drug Development in Oncology
key_points: Approvals based on biomarker rather than tumor site (e.g., NTRK fusion, MSI-H/dMMR, TMB-H)
Basket trial design required to demonstrate activity across tumor types
Central testing for biomarker (companion diagnostic) required
Response rate as primary endpoint acceptable for accelerated approval
Confirmatory evidence requirements across tumor types
Durability of response critical for regulatory assessment

ctdna_mrd.title: Circulating Tumor DNA (ctDNA) and Minimal Residual Disease (MRD) as Endpoints
key_points: ctDNA clearance as potential surrogate endpoint in select settings
MRD negativity accepted in haematologic malignancies (e.g., multiple myeloma, CLL)
Assay validation requirements: sensitivity, specificity, analytical validity
ctDNA for treatment monitoring and response assessment
FDA qualification pathway for ctDNA as drug development tool
Not yet accepted as sole primary endpoint for regular approval in solid tumors

gene_therapy_general.title: Human Gene Therapy Products Incorporating Human Genome Editing
key_points: Genome editing products: CRISPR-Cas, ZFN, TALEN-based therapies
Off-target analysis requirements: whole genome sequencing, unbiased methods
Long-term follow-up: minimum 15 years for integrating vectors, 5 years for non-integrating
Manufacturing considerations: potency assays, identity testing, sterility
Nonclinical requirements: biodistribution, tumorigenicity, immunogenicity
Clinical considerations: dose escalation design, patient selection, monitoring plan
protocol_requirements: Off-target editing assessment methodology specified
Long-term follow-up plan with specific monitoring endpoints
Dose escalation/de-escalation rules with safety stopping criteria
Immunogenicity monitoring plan (anti-vector and anti-transgene)
Patient eligibility based on prior gene therapy exposure

car_t_cell_therapy.title: Considerations for the Development of Chimeric Antigen Receptor (CAR) T Cell Products
key_points: Manufacturing: vein-to-vein process, turnaround time, cell identity/potency
Clinical considerations: lymphodepleting conditioning, cytokine release syndrome (CRS) management, neurotoxicity (ICANS)
CRS grading: ASTCT consensus grading system
REMS may be required for CRS/ICANS management
Long-term safety: secondary malignancies including T-cell lymphoma (FDA boxed warning class-wide 2024)
Bridging therapy during manufacturing allowance

regenerative_medicine.title: Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) Designation
key_points: Expedited development for regenerative medicine therapies (gene therapies, cell therapies, tissue engineering)
Early interactions with FDA for development planning
Eligibility for priority review and accelerated approval
Preliminary clinical evidence of potential to address unmet need required
Applies to gene therapies, therapeutic tissue engineering products, human cell/tissue products, combination products

biosimilar_development.title: Biosimilar Development and the BPCI Act
key_points: Totality of evidence approach for biosimilar approval
Stepwise approach: analytical → functional → animal → clinical (PK/PD → comparative efficacy/safety)
Clinical study may be waived if analytical and functional similarity is high
Interchangeability designation: automatic substitution at pharmacy level
Reference product designation requirements
Extrapolation of indications: biosimilar approved for one indication may be extrapolated to other approved indications of reference product
protocol_requirements: Comparative analytical characterisation vs reference product
PK equivalence study design: crossover or parallel, 80-125% bioequivalence range
Comparative clinical study (if required): non-inferiority or equivalence design
Immunogenicity comparative assessment: parallel design, 12+ months
Switching study for interchangeability: multiple switch crossover design

diversity_action_plans.title: Diversity Plans to Improve Enrollment of Participants from Underrepresented Populations in Clinical Studies
key_points: Required for Phase III and certain pivotal studies (as of 2024)
Must include enrollment goals for underrepresented populations
Underrepresented: based on disease epidemiology — sex, age, race, ethnicity
Sponsors must submit diversity plan as part of IND or with protocol
FDA can grant waivers for rare diseases or when diversity goals are impractical
Decentralised trial elements may improve diversity
Community engagement strategies required
Reporting: demographic data included in labeling and FDA review
protocol_requirements: Diversity enrollment targets specified in protocol
Recruitment strategies for underrepresented populations
Site selection strategy to access diverse populations
Language and cultural considerations for consent documents
Subgroup analyses by race, ethnicity, sex, age pre-specified

informed_consent_21cfr50.title: 21 CFR Part 50: Protection of Human Subjects — Informed Consent
key_points: 8 basic elements of informed consent (21 CFR 50.25(a)): purpose, procedures, risks, benefits, alternatives, confidentiality, compensation/treatment for injury, voluntary participation
6 additional elements when appropriate (21 CFR 50.25(b)): unforeseeable risks, circumstances for termination, additional costs, consequences of withdrawal, new findings disclosure, number of subjects
IRB-approved consent form required
Language understandable to subject
No exculpatory language permitted
eConsent allowed: 21 CFR Part 11 compliant, option for paper copy

irb_requirements_21cfr56.title: 21 CFR Part 56: Institutional Review Boards
key_points: IRB approval required before human subject enrollment
IRB composition: at least 5 members, diverse backgrounds, at least one non-scientist, at least one non-affiliated member
Criteria for approval: risks minimised, risks reasonable relative to benefits, equitable selection, informed consent, data monitoring, privacy protections, vulnerable population safeguards
Continuing review: at least annually
Expedited review categories for minimal risk changes
Suspension/termination authority for non-compliance or harm
